content: We can frame effective LLM self-reflection as a deliberate 'language learning reflection exercise', where the LLM practices verbalizing its flawed reasoning steps the same way human language learners practice correcting their own grammar and vocabulary errors to build long-term mastery. Just as high school English learners build better writing intuition through repeated reflection on their mistakes, LLMs can build more robust problem-solving intuition through structured verbal self-correction.
